context.brief: Help a first-time AI coding agent user understand design-ai and reach a verified install or quickstart path from the first viewport.

notes: The report records only local P14 evidence produced from the approved homepage pilot.
Repository mutation, deployment, and external adoption claims remain outside this report.

sources.observation: Captured the rendered documentation homepage before the approved implementation.
Recorded first-viewport, accessibility, responsive, and console observations from the local baseline.

lenses.summary: The first viewport explains distribution status before giving a first-time visitor a clear install or quickstart action.

evidence.observation: The badges and long release paragraph occupy the primary content area without a prominent next action.
The mobile first viewport does not expose a clear install or quickstart command.

lenses.summary: The baseline capture did not verify repeated search or navigation response.

evidence.observation: No repeated-action sequence was retained for the baseline.

lenses.summary: The baseline capture did not establish focus continuity through the skip link and primary action.

evidence.observation: Keyboard destination continuity was not proven before implementation.

lenses.summary: The baseline capture did not exercise repeated open and dismiss cycles.

evidence.observation: Search and drawer interruption behavior remained unverified.

lenses.summary: The baseline capture did not verify reduced-motion behavior.

evidence.observation: No reduced-motion profile was retained for the baseline.

lenses.summary: The baseline console recorded a failed Pretendard font request and the local language sitemap warning.

evidence.observation: The browser console recorded a 403 response from the previous Pretendard CDN URL and a local /ko/sitemap.xml 404.

lenses.summary: Static inspection confirmed an unnamed search toggle input.

evidence.observation: The canonical static review found one P1 accessible-name defect.

lenses.summary: The baseline did not overflow, but the mobile first viewport buried the next action below release status content.

evidence.observation: The 390 by 844 viewport renders without horizontal overflow while withholding the primary action from the first view.

findings.title: Expose one clear first-value path in the first viewport
findings.before: Badges and release status lead the page without a prominent install or quickstart action.
findings.after: Lead with a concise product promise, one primary quickstart action, one distribution action, and compact proof.
findings.why: A first-time visitor must understand the product and reach a verified starting path without scanning release metadata.

evidence.observation: The desktop baseline contains no prominent first-value action.
The mobile baseline hides the first useful action below the first viewport.

verification: Confirm the product promise and primary quickstart action are visible in the first desktop and mobile viewports.
Confirm the primary action points to the verified quickstart documentation path.

findings.title: Give the input control a programmatic name
findings.before: The input control has no associated label or supported accessible-name attribute.
findings.after: Associate a visible label, or provide an accurate aria-label or aria-labelledby reference.
findings.why: Screen-reader and voice-control users need a stable programmatic name for every interactive control.

evidence.observation: Static inspection found the search toggle input without a programmatic name.

verification: Inspect the accessibility tree and confirm the control exposes the intended name.
Reach the control by keyboard and confirm its visible focus indicator remains present.

summary.nextAction: Implement the approved first-value path and accessible header controls, then collect browser evidence at both declared viewports.
